page: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.48xlarge is a high-performance computing server powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 processors. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 96 physical cores and 96 virtual CPUs, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz with a single thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and features a baseline network bandwidth of 150 Gbps. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this server type.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10%. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth for large blocks, alongside strong PassMark memory scores. In computational tests, the server achieves top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and physics simulations. Single-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for Geekbench and stress-ng single-core tests.

The high core density and memory bandwidth make this server highly cost-efficient for parallel computing workloads that do not require local storage or GPU acceleration. It is optimized for high-performance computing tasks, large-scale scientific simulations, software compilation, and database operations. Additionally, the platform demonstrates top-tier prompt processing speeds for large language models, making it suitable for CPU-based LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc7a.48xlarge is a high-performance computing instance featuring 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors running at 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and runs on the Nitro hypervisor with 150 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. It does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and top-tier results in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations. This server is highly efficient for parallel computing, scientific simulations, and CPU-based large language model prompt processing.
